source.description:
IMPORTANT: This package version requires Studio v24.10 or above. Installing it on versions lower than the recommended ones may cause unexpected changes in your workflows.
Execute Excel related operations using the Open Office XML format (XLSX) or Excel.Interop.

activities.description: Copies an Excel range, table, or sheet, or table data saved for later, and pastes it in a specified CSV file either pasting it after existing data or replacing any existing data in the file.
Copies all entries from a specified CSV file to an Excel range, table, or sheet, or saves the data for later use in the automation.
Appends the data from a DataTable to a spreadsheet. If the sheet does not exist, a new one is created with the SheetName value. Changes are immediately saved
Creates a pivot table from a specified range or table to help you calculate, summarize, and analyze data.
Gets the color of a cell
Extracts the range of an Excel table from a specified spreadsheet
Reads the value of a cell from a spreadsheet as a string
Reads the formula of a cell from a spreadsheet as a string
Reads the values from a column beginning from the cell specified in the StartingCell field, and stores them in an IEnumerable <object>variable.

Reads the value of a range in Range Excel Format from a spreadsheet as a DataTable. If Range isn't specified, the whole spreadsheet is read. If range is specified as a cell, the whole spreadsheet starting from that cell is read
Reads the values of an Excel row beginning from the cell specified in the StartingCell field, and stores it in an IEnumerable<object> variable.
Sets the color of a range
Writes the Text value into a cell from a spreadsheet. If the sheet does not exist, a new one is created with the SheetName value. If a value exists, it is overwritten. Changes are immediately saved
Extracts the background color of a cell and saves it as a Color variable.
Copies the data in a table, range, or sheet and pastes it after existing data in another specified table, range, or sheet.
Fills cells with data based on data in other cells using the AutoFill feature in Excel.
Fits data in cells.
Change the Source range for a Pivot Table.
Clears the data from a spreadsheet sheet, range, or table.
Saves an Excel chart to an image file or to the clipboard for use in other activities.
Copies a range or sheet and optionally pastes it to another location in the current workbook or a different one.
Creates a pivot table from a specified range or table to help you calculate, summarize, and analyze data.
Formats a range of cells as a table with a specified name.
Deletes a specified column from a sheet, table or range.
Deletes the specified rows from a sheet, table, or range.
Deletes a specified sheet from an Excel file.
Creates a copy of the specified sheet in an Excel file.
Open or create an Excel file to use in your automation.
Repeats the contained activities once for each row in the specified sheet, range, or table.
Opens or reuses an Excel process. To be used in combination with the "Use Excel" activity to open Excel documents.
Executes a specified macro within a macro-enabled workbook.
Exports the specified range, table, pivotTable or sheet to a CSV file.
Enters a formula or value in all the cells in a range.
Creates a filter in a pivot table based on the values in a single column. Can also be used to clear existing filters.
Creates a filter in a range, table, or sheet based on the values in a single column. Can also be used to clear existing filters.
Finds the first and the last rows containing data in a given sheet, range or table.
Searches in a specified range for a certain value. Depending on the Operation chosen, it either returns the location of the cell or it replaces the value(s) with another given value.
Repeats actions for each sheet in an Excel workbook.
Sets the format for all the cells in a specified range.
Inserts a column in a sheet, table, or range at the specified location.
Create a chart in an Excel file.
Inserts one or more rows in a table, range, or sheet at the specified location.
Inserts a sheet in an Excel file.
Invokes a macro from an external file containing VBA code and runs it against an Excel file.
Finds data in a range or sheet using Excel's LOOKUP function.
Searches for a specified item in a range of cells, and then returns the relative position of that item in the range.
Protects a sheet in an Excel workbook.
Reads the formula from a spreadsheet cell.
Reads the value from a spreadsheet cell.
Reads the value of an Excel range as a DataTable.
Get the latest data by refreshing all sources in the workbook.
Updates the data for a pivot table.
Delete duplicate rows
Renames a sheet in an Excel file.
Saves an Excel file as a PDF file.
Save an Excel file as a different file.
Saves any pending changes in the selected Excel file.

Use this activity as root of your Attended Workflow to enforce that at most one Excel process exists at start of execution.  This behavior ensures all the Excel documents are open in the same Excel process and will make your workflow more stable and faster. This activity is used as root for StudioX workflows.
Sorts the data in a specified sheet, table, or range by one or more columns.
Splits the text from a cell, range, or table into different columns.
Unprotects a sheet in an Excel workbook.
Update properties of an existing Excel chart.
Finds data in a range or sheet using Excel's VLOOKUP function.
Enters a text or a formula in a cell.
Write Range is intended for writing data from external Data Table sources into Excel.

source.description:
IMPORTANT: This package version requires Studio v23.4 or above. Installing it on versions lower than the recommended ones may cause unexpected changes in your workflows.
Core activities which enable the robots to manipulate data tables and collections, work with files and folders. Package also contains workflow operators, dialog forms, debugging and invoking methods.

activities.description: Securely store and load usernames and passwords.
Pauses adding Business Transaction logs for the operations to the ongoing transaction.
Resumes adding Business Transaction logs for the operations to the ongoing transaction.

Initiates any type of job (Agent, Agentic process, RPA workflows, API workflow, Testing process, etc.) via Orchestrator to be picked up by any available robots. It allows for flexible configuration of the execution mode (Do not wait, Wait for job completion or Suspend execution until job completion). It returns output arguments unless started in the Do not wait mode.
Sends an email notification via the Orchestrator API
Use this activity to download any file from a given URL.
Allows to add or subtract a specified unit of time from the source date.
Format the source DateTime variable in a text (string).
Select the desired letter case to convert the text to
Combines text from a given collection, with the provided separator

activities.displayName: Extract Date and Time from Text
activities.description: Extracts date and time from the given input text matching the specified format and outputs it as a variable.
Extracts text from the given input matching the specified criteria. The following extraction options are available: value between two strings, e-mail addresses, urls, and text from HTML code.
Search for a specified text and replace all occurrences with a new text.
Split the source text into a list of substrings based on a specified separator
Appends one or more items at the end of the specified collection.
Create a collection of items that have the same type as the first specified element.
Checks if the given Item exists in the collection
Filter a collection based on custom conditions
Combine collections in a new collection that contains all the elements
Remove the specified element from a collection
Adds an item at the end of the specified list.
Creates a new empty list.
Displays an HTML file that sends data back to the workflow.
Displays a dialog box that prompts the user with a label message and an input field.
Displays a message box with a specified text and buttons.
Opens a dialog box at runtime to browse for a file and select it to use in the task.
Opens a dialog box at runtime to select a folder to use later.
Generates a simple tone on the speaker.
Terminates a specified process.
Copies a specified folder to another location
Deletes a specified file.
Deletes a specified folder.
Checks if the specified file exists and returns True if the file exists, False if it does not.
Checks if the specified folder exists and returns True if the folder exists, False if it does not.
Repeats the contained activities once for each file in a specified folder.
Repeats the contained activities once for each child folder in the specified folder.
Retrieves the properties of a specified file and saves the information for later use in the project.
Retrieves the properties of a specified folder and saves the information for later use in the project.
Moves a specified folder to another location.
Renames the selected file
Renames the selected folder.
Monitors changes on a specified file or folder.
Executes a PowerShell command with specified input parameters.
Change the data type of a variable.
Trigger which fires when a global variable's value has changed.
Forces a global variable changed notification to be sent to all subscribers.
A container activity that allows management of triggers and handler activities.
Executes a specified VBScript, optionally passing it a list of input parameters.
Monitors termination of a specified Windows process.
Monitors launch of a specified Windows process.
Invokes a method of a specified COM object.
Sets multiple variables or arguments values in one activity
Updates a text value using modifications including find and replace, trim, and combining (concatentating) with another text value.
Else If activity chooses between different paths based on a series of conditions being true or false.
Creates a DataTable according to a specified schema.
Converts a Collection to a DataTable.

activities.displayName: For Each Row in Data Table
activities.description: Executes an action once for each row in the DataTable provided.
Gets the path to the specified system special folder.
Gets the contents of the specified environment variable.
Gets the list of all running processes.
Set Environment Variable
Copies a specified file to another location.
Creates a folder in the specified location.
Creates a file in the specified location.
Deletes the file or folder in the specified location.
Checks if the specified path exists. The path can represent a file path or a directory path.
Copies all the characters from a specified text file.
Copies a text and pastes it in a text file replacing any existing data in the file.
Helps you add custom alerts in Orchestrator, with a selected severity.
Performs HTTP requests to the Orchestrator API by authenticating under the robot it is executed on.
Gets a specified secret asset by using a provided Asset Name. If the secret asset is not global, it must be assigned to the local robot in order to be retrieved.
Gets a specified asset by using a provided AssetName. If the asset is not global, it must be assigned to the local robot in order to be retrieved.
Gets a specified credential asset by using a provided Asset Name. If the credential asset is not global, it must be assigned to the local robot in order to be retrieved.
Updates the value of an indicated asset, that is already available in Orchestrator, be it a global or a Per Robot asset.
Updates the value of an indicated secret asset, that is already available in Orchestrator, be it a global or a Per Robot asset.
Updates the value of an indicated credential asset, that is already available in Orchestrator, be it a global or a Per Robot asset.
Retrieves a list of Orchestrator jobs according to a custom filter, using the Orchestrator API.
Initiates a new job via Orchestrator to be picked up by any available robots. It immediately continues the execution without waiting for the job to finish. It does not return output arguments.
Stops or kills a job on Orchestrator.
Adds a new item in the queue. The status of the item will be New.
Adds a new item in the queue and starts a transaction. The status of the item will be set to InProgress. Returns the item as a QueueItem variable.
Adds a collection of items from a Data Table to a queue. The status of the items will be New.
Deletes items with the New state from a specified queue.
Gets an item from the queue so that you can process it (start the transaction) and sets its status to In Progress.
Retrieves a list of transactions from an indicated queue, according to multiple filters, such as creation date, priority, state and reference.
Adds time parameters between which a transaction must be processed (not before Postpone Date and no later than Deadline Date).
Start a job when a new item is added to the specified queue.
Helps you create custom progress statuses for your In Progress transactions.
Sets the status of a transaction item to Failed or Successful.
Waits for an item in the queue to be available so that you can process it (start the transaction) and sets its status to In Progress.
Checks if Stop was triggered in UiPath Orchestrator.
Ends the current workflow and resumes the process immediately after the point where the workflow was invoked. Compatible with Robots version 23.8 and above.
Creates a scope with limited execution time. It throws a System.TimeoutException in case of timeout.
Adds custom log fields to each Log Message.

Starts the specified process within the current robot execution. It does not wait for the invoked process to finish before resuming the execution. It does not return output arguments. It only works in attended scenarios.
Exits the Loop activity (For Each, While, Do While) or Trigger Scope activity in which it is placed and continues the workflow execution with the activity that follows it
Skips the current iteration in a Loop activity (For Each, While, Do While) and continues the execution with the next iteration.
Starts the specified process within the current robot execution. It waits for the invoked process to finish before resuming the execution. It returns output arguments.
Synchronously invokes a specified workflow, optionally passing it a list of input arguments.
Performs an activity or a series of activities on each element of an enumeration.
Associates a specific format to a generic value that can be used with .ToString and Parse operations. The available formats are Number, DateTime, Currency and Percentage.
Executes contained activities first and then loops if the condition is True
Executes contained activities while the condition is True
Writes the specified diagnostic message at the specified level. These messages are also sent to Orchestrator and displayed in the Logs page.
Removes custom log fields to each Log Message.
Displays a custom status in the UiPath Assistant for the process that is running.
Retries the contained activities as long as the condition is not met or an error is thrown.
Enables a local trigger.
Disables a local trigger.
This trigger will fire an event after each interval.
This activity initialises and starts all local triggers that listen for events on the user’s machine.
This activity stops all local triggers that listen for events on the user’s machine.
Contains job info such as Process name, Workflow name, User Name, User Email and Timestamp.
Schedule a recurrent time to start a job.
Evaluate Business Rule
Sets the status of the current Process Trace and close it. Do not set a status if the Process Trace extends beyond this Task scope.
Sets the status of a Task.
Adds a new object to the task. This object will appear as metadata information on the task in Process Mining.

Enables Process Tracking. This will track the execution of a Sequence as a Task in Process Mining.  You can configure this scope using properties panel and control how the execution gets tracked as part of a Task and Process within Process Mining.

source.description:
IMPORTANT: This package version requires Studio v2020.4 or above, as well as an Orchestrator instance running v2020.4 or above. Installing it on versions lower than the recommended ones may cause unexpected behavior.
Testing activities which enable users to verify expression values and control states in test case workflows.

activities.description: Attach Document to the current Test Case in Orchestrator
Compares two documents to determine their equivalence while allowing for specific sections to be excluded based on predefined rules.
Compares two texts to determine their equivalence while allowing for specific sections to be excluded based on predefined rules.

Activity that can create a flexible comparison rules designed to match and exclude specific parts of the text during comparison. The rule can use various matching techniques, such as regular expressions (regex) or wildcards. These rules allow the identification of patterns (e.g., dates, IDs, or dynamic content) that should be ignored, improving the accuracy and relevance of the comparison.

activities.displayName: Bulk Add Test Data Queue Items
activities.description: Add test data items to a queue in bulk
Delete the provided test data queue items
Get next test data item from a queue
Gets all the items from the specified queue
Add test data item to a queue
Verifies an expression
Verifies 2 expressions with an operator
Verify a specified value against a range.
Provide random address as dictionary
Provide a random first name
Provide a random last name
Random Date
Random Number
Generate a random string
Picks a random line in range of 0 - 2 billions from a 1-column list provided by the customer as .txt or .csv file

source.description:
IMPORTANT: This package version requires Studio 23.4 or higher. On lower versions some functionality may be missing, or unexpected behaviours may occur.

Package contains core activities which enable the automation of desktop applications, browsers, and virtual machines. Please check the documentation for more details.

activities.description: Selects, clears or toggles check boxes. Only CheckBox type elements are supported.
Clicks a specified UI element which is targeted using UiPath Computer Vision.
Searches for a specified UI element on the screen in the foreground using UiPath Computer Vision, and returns a Boolean variable that states whether it was found or not.
Extracts a table visible on the screen.
Extracts text from a specified position based on an anchor.
Visually highlights a specified UI element identified using UiPath Computer Vision.
Hovers over a specified UI element which is targeted by using UiPath Computer Vision.
A container that enables Computer Vision on a window or element. Also acts as a refresh mechanism.
Specifies the item to be selected from a dropdown list.
Sends keystrokes to a UI element. Special keys are supported and can be selected from the drop-down list. The UI element is identified using UiPath Computer Vision.
Invokes an Internet Explorer ActiveX object automation method.
Enables you to activate a specified UI element.
Exports all available UI elements
Sets the clipping region of a UI element.
Sets keyboard focus to a specified UI element.
Retrieves the bounding rectangle of a specified UI element.
Selects one or more options from a multiple options list box. After you indicate the element on the screen, use MultipleItems property to define the options that will be selected at runtime.
Sets the value of a specified web attribute.
Waits for the value of a specified UI element attribute to be equal to a string.
Retrieves a collection of children UI elements according to a specified scope and filter criteria.
Searches for a UI element by using a position relative to a fixed element. This should be used when a reliable selector is not available.
Retrieves an ancestor for a specified UI element.
Enables the user to select an UI element or region on screen while running the workflow.
Waits for the specified UI element to appear on the screen and returns it as a UiElement variable.
Searches for matches for a specific image in a target UI element and returns a collection of UI elements which have the clipping region set to the screen position of the matches.
Enables you to load an image from the disk and save it to an Image variable.
Enables you to save an image to your disk.
Injects .NET code into target process.

Extracts a string and its information from an indicated UI element using the Google Cloud Vision OCR engine. It can be used with other OCR activities (Click OCR Text, Hover OCR Text, Double Click OCR Text, Get OCR Text, Find OCR Text Position).

Extracts a string and its information from an indicated UI element using Tesseract OCR Engine. It can be used with other OCR activities (Click OCR Text, Hover OCR Text, Double Click OCR Text, Get OCR Text, Find OCR Text Position).

Extracts a string and its information from an indicated UI element using the Microsoft Azure Computer Vision OCR engine. It can be used with other OCR activities (Click OCR Text, Hover OCR Text, Double Click OCR Text, Get OCR Text, Find OCR Text Position).

Extracts a string and its information from an indicated UI element using the OCR screen scraping method. This activity can also be automatically generated when performing screen scraping, along with a container.
Retrieves the current active Window and enables you to perform multiple actions within it.
Hides the indicated window. This makes the window completely invisible.
Maximizes the indicated window.
Minimizes the indicated window.
Changes the position and dimensions of the indicated window.
Restores a specified window to its original size and position.
Displays and activates the specified window.
Uses AI to seamlessly extract form data.
Uses AI to seamlessly populate the designated form with information extracted from the provided data source.
Uses AI to seamlessly update an UI element’s state/value.
Performs a given UI task, using AI, on the attached application.
Opens a desktop application or web browser page to use in your automation.
Captures and handles browser dialogs such as alert, confirm and prompt.
Captures and handles a browser file picker dialog.
Used to check, uncheck, or toggle a checkbox.
Checks if an element is enabled or disabled.
Ensures the automated app is in a specific state, by verifying if a UI element exists or not. A set of user-defined actions are executed based on the detected state.
Clicks a specified UI element.
Setup a click event trigger on the indicated UI Element.
Dismisses all popups that are on top of the application and block a target, using the configured close buttons.
Executes a drag and drop operation from the source UI element to the destination UI element.
Extracts tabular data from a specified web page or application.
Iterates over a structured set of UiElements.
Retrieves the value of a specified attribute of the indicated UI element.
Retrieves the value of a specified attribute of the indicated UI element.
Exports the session data from the specified browser instance.
Gets the system's Clipboard data.
Extracts the text from a specified UI element.
Returns the URL from the current browser.
Navigates to the specified URL in the indicated web browser.
Visually highlights a specified UI element by surrounding it in a box.
Hovers the mouse over a specified UI element.
Executes JavaScript code in the context of the web page corresponding to a UiElement.
Sends one or more keyboard shortcuts to a UI element.
Setup a key pressed event trigger on the indicated UI Element.
Sends mouse scroll events to the specified UI element.
Setup a trigger on a given event on the indicated UI Element.
Allows basic navigation of the browser, like Go back, Go forward, Close, Refresh, Home.
Executes a transaction code or program in the current SAP GUI window.
Clicks the picture displayed on a SAP GUI screen.
Clicks a button from the system or application SAP toolbars. After indicating the SAP Toolbar on the screen, the list with all available buttons is displayed in the activity.

Use the activity to identify any cell inside SAP ALV Hierarchical Table. After the identification of the cell all typical UI activities can be performed, such as Click, Double Click, Get Text and others.
Expand parent ALV tree to the selected node. After indicating the SAP node element from the SAP GUI window, the selected node path will be displayed in the activity.
Expand parent tree to an active node or an active item. After indicating the SAP Tree element from the SAP GUI window, the Tree list with all available nodes and items is displayed in the activity.
Use the activity to log into an SAP system.
Use the activity to directly log on to an SAP system. Provide the exact SAP connection name from the SAP Logon or the SAP Logon Pad window used to log on to your SAP system.
Reads the message displayed in the Status Bar on the bottom of the SAP GUI window.
Select Dates in Calendar. The activity can be used to select single dates or periods of time.
Select a Menu Item from the main SAP GUI window. After indicating the window, the list with all available Menu Items is displayed in the activity.
A container that enables you to attach to an existing Table UI element and perform multiple actions within it.
Selects an item from a drop-down list.
Imports the session data into a specified browser instance.
Sets the system's Clipboard data to the given text.
Sets keyboard focus to the specified UI element.
Sets the currently active runtime browser.
Enters text in a specified UI element, for example a text box.
Takes a screenshot of an application or UI element.
Enters text in a specified UI element, for example a text box.
Suppress keyboard/mouse input until the set key combination is pressed, or timeout exceeded.
Unblock keyboard/mouse input that has been previously blocked by a 'Block User Input' activity.
Perform various operations on the specified window element.

source.description: Unofficial port of the Windows Workflow Foundation (WF) to .NET 6
